beginner.question: How do you create a navigation bar with Flexbox?
beginner.answer: Use display: flex on the nav container and gap for spacing; each link is a flex item.

beginner.question: How do you create a card grid with Flexbox?
beginner.answer: Use display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap on the container, with flex: 0 0 calc(33.33% - gap) on each card.

beginner.question: How do you create a sticky footer with Flexbox?
beginner.answer: Set body { display: flex; flex-direction: column; min-height: 100vh; } and main { flex: 1; }.

beginner.question: What is the Holy Grail layout in Flexbox?
beginner.answer: The Holy Grail has header, three-column middle (nav, main, sidebar), and footer, all using flex properties.

beginner.question: How do you create a media object with Flexbox?
beginner.answer: A media object has a fixed-width image on the left and flexible content on the right using display: flex.

beginner.question: How do you center content with Flexbox?
beginner.answer: Use display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center on the container.

beginner.question: How do you create equal-height columns with Flexbox?
beginner.answer: Use display: flex on the row container; columns automatically have equal height due to align-items: stretch.

beginner.question: How do you create a horizontal scrollable container with Flexbox?
beginner.answer: Use display: flex; overflow-x: auto; flex-wrap: nowrap on the container.

beginner.question: How do you create a centered content wrapper with Flexbox?
beginner.answer: Use display: flex; justify-content: center on the container with a max-width child.

beginner.question: How do you reverse the order of items in Flexbox?
beginner.answer: Use flex-direction: row-reverse or column-reverse to reverse item order.

intermediate.question: How do you build the Holy Grail layout with min-height: 100vh?
intermediate.answer: Use body { display: flex; flex-direction: column; min-height: 100vh; }, a main row with flex: 1, and a footer.

intermediate.question: How do you create a responsive navbar that becomes vertical on mobile?
intermediate.answer: Use flex-direction: row on desktop and switch to column in a media query at the mobile breakpoint.

intermediate.question: How do you create a centered card grid that wraps?
intermediate.answer: Use display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; justify-content: center; gap: 20px; with fixed-width or minmax cards.

intermediate.question: How do you build a media object with the image on the right?
intermediate.answer: Use display: flex on the container and order: 1 on the image, or use flex-direction: row-reverse.

intermediate.question: How do you create a sidebar layout that collapses on mobile?
intermediate.answer: Use flex: 0 0 250px for the sidebar and flex: 1 for content; hide sidebar in media queries.

intermediate.question: How do you create a dashboard layout with Flexbox?
intermediate.answer: Use nested flex containers: a main vertical stack with header/content/footer and a horizontal content area with sidebar.

intermediate.question: How do you distribute navigation items evenly across the bar?
intermediate.answer: Use justify-content: space-between or space-evenly on the flex nav container.

intermediate.question: How do you create a form with Flexbox row layout?
intermediate.answer: Use display: flex; gap: 10px; on the form, with the submit button having flex: 0 0 auto and inputs flex: 1.

intermediate.question: How do you create a flexible pricing card layout?
intermediate.answer: Use display: flex; justify-content: center; gap: 20px; with individual cards as flex items and flex: 1.

intermediate.question: How do you create a split content layout (50/50)?
intermediate.answer: Use display: flex with two children, each having flex: 1 or width: 50%.

advanced.question: How do you build a pinned header and sidebar using Flexbox?
advanced.answer: Use position: sticky with top: 0 on the header and sidebar within a flex layout for pinned behavior.

advanced.question: How do you create a responsive layout that switches from column to row at a breakpoint?
advanced.answer: Use flex-direction: column by default and @media (min-width: 768px) { .container { flex-direction: row; } }.

advanced.question: How do you implement a CSS-only tab layout using Flexbox?
advanced.answer: Use flexbox for tabs (equal distribution) and :target or checkbox hack to show/hide tab content panels.

advanced.question: How do you create a dashboard with cards that have different flex-grow values?
advanced.answer: Assign different flex: values to cards based on importance (e.g., flex: 2 for primary, flex: 1 for secondary).

advanced.question: How do you create a footer that stays at the bottom but scrolls with long content?
advanced.answer: Use flex: 1 on main and min-height: 100vh on body; the footer sits at bottom when content is short.

advanced.question: How do you build a complex navigation with nested flex containers?
advanced.answer: Use an outer flex container for the full navbar, inner flex containers for menu sections, and absolute flex for dropdowns.

advanced.question: How do you implement a responsive media object that stacks on mobile?
advanced.answer: Use flex-direction: row on desktop and flex-direction: column in a media query to stack image above text.

advanced.question: How do you create a flex-based layout that respects reading order for accessibility?
advanced.answer: Keep DOM order logical and use flex properties for visual arrangement; avoid reordering with the order property.

advanced.question: How do you create an auto-filling gallery using flex shorthand?
advanced.answer: Use flex: 0 0 300px; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 10px; items will auto-fill rows with 300px items and wrap.

advanced.question: How do you build a complete page layout with header, hero, content, sidebar, and footer using nested flexes?
advanced.answer: Use a top-level column flex for page sections, row flex for content+sidebar, and individual flex containers for each section.
